name: Frequency Deep-Characterization
carving_goal: Remove the ambiguity around --freq. Is it resonance, amplitude, coupling, or null?
I_c_skip_reason: PRD rule: freq=1.0 at Phase H gave 20% HIGH < 60% threshold, so coupling test of a non-effect is not warranted.

I_a_harmonic.basin_value_note: HIGH E always in [87.9, 89.2], LOW E in [74.2, 76.3] regardless of freq — basin LOCATIONS are freq-independent

I_b_holography.holography_basin_switching: NONE — all 25 episodes in RETRY cluster (E ∈ [13.7, 15.9], Coh ∈ [0.710, 0.768])
I_b_holography.freq_affects_holography_attractor: NO — E and Coh distributions are indistinguishable across the 5 freq values

categorization_of_freq.reasoning: Across 50 harmonic episodes, HIGH rate varies from 20%-80% per config. No single clean peak. The 4/5 counts at freq=0.8 and freq=1.5 could be binomial noise at N=5 (binomial std ~1 ep at p=0.44). There is a suggestive 'elevated band' from 0.8-2.0 (mean HIGH rate ~60%) vs edges (0.1, 0.3, 5.0, 10.0 avg ~30%), but statistical significance at N=5 per config is marginal. Definitely NOT a single resonance peak. Definitely NOT monotone amplitude. Weakly consistent with 'band-coupling' — freq values in an intermediate range interact with the dynamics more than extremes.
categorization_of_freq.freq_1_0_specialness: NONE. freq=1.0 actually gave the LOWEST HIGH rate (1/5) of all tested values — NOT the peak. Session 3's '100% HIGH at freq=1.0' was definitively 2-episode noise.
categorization_of_freq.basin_locations_insensitive: HIGH basin E=88-89 and LOW basin E=74-76 at EVERY freq value. The basin SELECTION probability varies but basin LOCATIONS do not. Confirms freq does not deform the attractor landscape — it just (weakly) biases selection.

resonance_peak_near_1.observed: 1.0 is a DIP, not a peak

multi_peaked_coupling.observed: two apparent maxes at 0.8 and 1.5, no clean spectral-mode assignment

null_effect.observed: std_high_pct = 21.2 across 10 points — not flat, but not a clear curve either

findings.F_I_1: --freq is NOT a resonance parameter at single frequencies 0.1-10.0. No clean peak exists.
findings.F_I_2: freq=1.0 is NOT special — in fact it showed 20% HIGH, among the lowest values tested.
findings.F_I_3: Basin values (HIGH E=88-89, LOW E=74-76) are freq-independent. Only basin-selection probability varies weakly.
findings.F_I_4: Holography task's RETRY attractor is completely insensitive to --freq across 25 episodes (5 values × 5 eps). No basin-switching observed.
findings.F_I_5: Weak suggestion of elevated-HIGH band around freq=[0.8, 2.0] but N=5 per point is too few to claim significance.

session_3_revision.spectral_resonance_hypothesis: UNSUPPORTED at tested range — mode-1 (~0.033) and mode-10 (~0.33) predicted strong resonance. Observed: freq=0.3 gave 20% HIGH, no peak near spectral modes.

implications_for_downstream_phases.phase_J: freq arm of holography sweep will likely show no variation — but already confirmed here. Can still run for completeness.
implications_for_downstream_phases.phase_K_L: freq-dependence is small and noise-dominated; asymmetry remains the primary order parameter to investigate in K and L.
